title: How to Read a Book: The Classic Guide to Intelligent Reading
author: Mortimer J. Adler, Charles Van Doren
excerpt: The timeless guide to reading books that matter—teaching four levels of reading from elementary to syntopical, and showing that the art of reading well is the foundation of all genuine learning.
keyIdeas: Reading is an active, not passive, activity that requires effort and skill
There are four levels of reading: elementary, inspectional, analytical, and syntopical
Analytical reading involves classifying, outlining, and criticizing a book
Syntopical reading synthesizes multiple books on the same subject
keyTakeaways: Inspectionally read every book before committing to analytical reading
Ask four questions of every book: what is it about? what is being said in detail? is it true? what of it?
Mark up your books with marginal notes to engage actively with the text
Read syntopically across multiple books to develop deep understanding of a subject
whoShouldRead: Anyone who wants to get more from the books they read
Self-directed learners building their own education
Students who want to develop critical reading skills
Readers of this knowledge management system who want to maximize value from every book
whoShouldSkip: Those looking for speed-reading techniques or shortcuts
Readers who prefer light, pleasure reading over analytical engagement
